englishDefinitions: (intransitive) To escape, especially forcefully or defiantly.
(transitive) To rescue someone or aid their escape as in sense 1.
(transitive, idiomatic) To bring out from storage, use, or present.
(transitive) To take or force out by breaking.
(intransitive) To begin suddenly; to emerge in a certain condition.
(intransitive, of a person) To suddenly get pimples or a rash, especially on one's face.
(especially recording industry, intransitive) (Of a record, product, or company): to achieve widespread success.
(obsolete, New England) To remove snow from a road or sidewalk.
persianTranslation: (غیر گذرا) فرار به خصوص به زور یا سرکشی.
(گذرا) نجات دادن کسی یا کمک به فرار او به معنای 1.
(متعدی، اصطلاحی) بیرون آوردن از انبار، استفاده یا حاضر کردن.
(متعدی) با شکستن بیرون آوردن یا مجبور کردن.
(غیر گذرا) برای شروع ناگهانی; در یک شرایط خاص ظهور کند.
(غیر متعدی، شخص) به طور ناگهانی جوش یا راش مخصوصاً روی صورت.
(به ویژه صنعت ضبط، غیرقابل انتقال) (از یک رکورد، محصول یا شرکت): برای دستیابی به موفقیت گسترده.
(منسوخ، نیوانگلند) برای حذف برف از جاده یا پیاده رو.
examples: They broke out of prison in the middle of the night.
The brigade succeeded in breaking out of the pocket and reunited with friendly forces.
After Big Tony was sent to prison, his friends got together to break him out.
Break out the bubbly and celebrate.
Before you immediately break out calculus, consider that there might be a more elegant way to find the answer in this case.

Picks and shovels had been perfectly adequate for the sporadic digging we’d had to do over the last month, but when faced with 30 cubic yards of earth to move in at once, we decided to break out the heavy equipment.
Break out the cables from the harness once they are inside the frame.
to break out a pane of glass
The First World War broke out in 1914.
It wasn't until later that her album broke out and she hit the big time.
Art. 24—To see what action the town will take in relation to breaking out highways and what portion of the road money, if any, shall be reserved for this purpose.
